# Arlopass

> Open-source AI access management for the web.

Arlopass is a free, open-source browser extension and developer SDK that lets web applications use a user's own AI providers — Ollama, Claude, GPT, Gemini, Bedrock — without the application ever seeing, touching, or storing the user's API keys.

## Key Facts

- **Category:** AI Access Management — the user-controlled layer between web apps and AI providers
- **License:** MIT (fully open source)
- **Price:** Free (users bring their own AI subscriptions)
- **Platforms:** Chrome browser extension, npm SDK (@arlopass/web-sdk)
- **Supported Providers:** Ollama, Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Perplexity, Microsoft Foundry
- **Developer Integration:** ~10 lines of code with connect(), createChat(), chat.stream()
- **Key Security Feature:** API keys encrypted in a local vault file (AES-256-GCM), secured by master password or OS keychain — never in the browser or on servers
- **Enterprise Features:** Zero-trust architecture, Ed25519-signed policy bundles, JSONL + OTLP audit trails, SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / ISO 42001 / GDPR control alignment

## How It Works

1. User installs the Arlopass browser extension (30 seconds, no account needed)
2. User connects their AI providers (keys encrypted in local vault)
3. When a web app wants to use AI, the user sees what it needs, picks a model, and approves the connection. The app then uses their AI seamlessly.

## For Developers

Developers install @arlopass/web-sdk and use three functions:
- connect() — establish session with user's extension
- createChat() — create a conversation
- chat.stream() or chat.send() — exchange messages

The user's Arlopass handles provider selection, authentication, and routing. Developers never see, store, or manage API keys.

## Links

- Homepage: https://arlopass.com
- Documentation: https://docs.arlopass.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/arlopass
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/arlopass
- Chrome Web Store: https://chrome.google.com/webstore
